verdict: PASS — Reading is 1893 words (within deep 1800–2200 budget, mermaid + code fences excluded), reading-aloud ~8.2 min. All three primary citations [1] W3Schools, [2] docs.python.org datastructures, [3] freeCodeCamp slicing are used inline with a matching bibliography and no orphans or fabrications. No forward references (tuples/sets/dicts/iterators never appear — the reading omits even the acceptable tuple aside). Faithful to corpus across all sampled assertions with zero unsupported claims. Beginner tone assuming only P1/P2. Diagram embedded as a ```mermaid block identical to diagram.mmd with a title caption; embeds_diagram=true. format_ok=true.

checks.name: Format discipline (no frontmatter, sidecar, six fixed sections)
checks.notes: First line is '# Lists' H1, no leading YAML frontmatter. reading.meta.json present and parses with word_count, estimated_read_minutes, citations_used, sections. Exactly six '##' sections in order: Overview, Key Concepts, Worked Example, In Practice, Key Takeaways, References.

checks.name: Length within deep budget 1800–2200 words
checks.notes: 1893 prose words (mermaid block and python code fences excluded per profile). Sidecar word_count=1893 consistent. Within budget; not under, not over.

checks.name: Citations used, matched, no fabrications
checks.notes: [1] W3Schools, [2] docs.python.org datastructures, [3] freeCodeCamp slicing all cited inline and all appear in the References bibliography. No orphan markers, no orphan bibliography entries, no fabricated sources.

checks.name: Prereq alignment / no forward references
checks.notes: P1/P2 vocab (variables, types, operators, f-strings, print, conditionals, loops, functions, range) used as known — allowed. No reference to later topics: 'tuple' does not appear at all (reading dropped the optional aside), and no sets/dicts (week 5) or iterators/generators/Counter/defaultdict. Hard-gate criteria clear.

checks.name: Faithfulness to corpus
checks.notes: Sampled 10 assertions — square-bracket literals (corpus 5.1), zero/negative indexing + IndexError (5.2), slice start:stop:step and a[::-1] (5.3), mutability + reference sharing b=a (5.4), eight methods in add/remove/search groups and append-vs-extend nesting (5.5), sort() returns None vs sorted() with reverse=/key= (5.7), nested grid[0][2] (5.8), comprehension map/filter/both (5.9). All trace to corpus. Worked Example and In Practice mirror corpus sections 9 and 7.

checks.name: Beginner plainspoken tone
checks.notes: Average sentence length under 25 words; no hedging phrases ('generally considered', 'many experts believe', 'various studies'). Direct, concrete, assumes no programming beyond P1/P2.

checks.name: Diagram embedded with caption, consistent with diagram.mmd
checks.notes: A ```mermaid flowchart block (reading.md lines 22–44) is byte-identical to artifacts/diagram.mmd, carries a title caption 'Indexing: 0..n-1 from the left, -1..-n from the right', and is introduced by explanatory prose (line 20). Sidecar embeds_diagram=true.

checks.name: Project format check (underlined bold labels + bulleted enumerations)
checks.notes: All nine Key Concepts sub-topics lead with '<u>**Label.**</u>' (Creating a list, Indexing, Slicing, Mutability, List methods, Iteration, Sorting, Nested lists, Comprehensions). Run-together enumerations are rendered as Markdown bullet lists: list methods split into three bulleted groups (add/remove/search), sort vs sorted as bullets, reverse/key as bullets, comprehension map/filter/both as bullets. No enumeration buried in prose.
